package cmd

import (
	"bytes"
	"os"
	"os/exec"
	"path/filepath"
	"testing"

	gitpkg "github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/git"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
)

func TestVersion_StaleWarning_PrintsToStderr(t *testing.T) {
	t.Setenv("DDX_DISABLE_UPDATE_CHECK", "1")

	repoDir := initVersionTestRepo(t, "https://github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx.git")
	initialSHA := gitCommitFile(t, repoDir, "README.md", "# initial\n", "initial")
	_ = gitCommitFile(t, repoDir, "notes.txt", "stale\n", "stale")

	stdout, stderr, err := runVersionCommand(t, repoDir, "v1.2.3", initialSHA)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	assert.Contains(t, stdout, "DDx v1.2.3")
	assert.Contains(t, stderr, "WARNING: installed ddx is built from")
	assert.Contains(t, stderr, "commits ahead")
	assert.Contains(t, stderr, "Run \"make install\" to refresh.")
}

func TestVersion_NoWarningWhenInSync(t *testing.T) {
	t.Setenv("DDX_DISABLE_UPDATE_CHECK", "1")

	repoDir := initVersionTestRepo(t, "https://github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx.git")
	headSHA := gitCommitFile(t, repoDir, "README.md", "# initial\n", "initial")

	_, stderr, err := runVersionCommand(t, repoDir, "v1.2.3", headSHA)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	assert.Empty(t, stderr)
}

func TestVersion_NoWarningOutsideDDxRepo(t *testing.T) {
	t.Setenv("DDX_DISABLE_UPDATE_CHECK", "1")

	repoDir := initVersionTestRepo(t, "https://github.com/example/not-ddx.git")
	initialSHA := gitCommitFile(t, repoDir, "README.md", "# initial\n", "initial")
	_ = gitCommitFile(t, repoDir, "notes.txt", "stale\n", "stale")

	_, stderr, err := runVersionCommand(t, repoDir, "v1.2.3", initialSHA)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	assert.Empty(t, stderr)
}

func TestVersion_HandlesDetachedHead(t *testing.T) {
	t.Setenv("DDX_DISABLE_UPDATE_CHECK", "1")

	repoDir := initVersionTestRepo(t, "https://github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx.git")
	initialSHA := gitCommitFile(t, repoDir, "README.md", "# initial\n", "initial")
	headSHA := gitCommitFile(t, repoDir, "notes.txt", "detached\n", "detached")

	detach := exec.Command("git", "checkout", "--detach", headSHA)
	detach.Dir = repoDir
	detach.Env = gitpkg.CleanEnv()
	out, err := detach.CombinedOutput()
	require.NoError(t, err, "git checkout --detach: %s", string(out))

	stdout, stderr, err := runVersionCommand(t, repoDir, "v1.2.3", initialSHA)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	assert.Contains(t, stdout, "DDx v1.2.3")
	assert.NotEmpty(t, stderr)
}

func initVersionTestRepo(t *testing.T, originURL string) string {
	t.Helper()

	repoDir := t.TempDir()
	runVersionGit(t, repoDir, "init")
	runVersionGit(t, repoDir, "config", "user.email", "test@example.com")
	runVersionGit(t, repoDir, "config", "user.name", "Test User")
	runVersionGit(t, repoDir, "remote", "add", "origin", originURL)
	return repoDir
}

func gitCommitFile(t *testing.T, repoDir, relPath, content, commitMsg string) string {
	t.Helper()

	fullPath := filepath.Join(repoDir, relPath)
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(fullPath), 0o755))
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(fullPath, []byte(content), 0o644))
	runVersionGit(t, repoDir, "add", relPath)
	runVersionGit(t, repoDir, "commit", "-m", commitMsg)
	return runVersionGitOutput(t, repoDir, "rev-parse", "HEAD")
}

func runVersionCommand(t *testing.T, workDir, version, commit string) (string, string, error) {
	t.Helper()

	factory := NewCommandFactory(workDir)
	factory.Version = version

    87	func gitCommitFile(t *testing.T, repoDir, relPath, content, commitMsg string) string {
    88		t.Helper()
    89	
    90		fullPath := filepath.Join(repoDir, relPath)
    91		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(fullPath), 0o755))
    92		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(fullPath, []byte(content), 0o644))
    93		runVersionGit(t, repoDir, "add", relPath)
    94		runVersionGit(t, repoDir, "commit", "-m", commitMsg)
    95		return runVersionGitOutput(t, repoDir, "rev-parse", "HEAD")
    96	}

    12	// TestCheckBinaryInstallLocationDetectsStaleCopy verifies that a fake ddx binary
    13	// shadowed earlier on PATH (with different content/SHA-256) is reported as stale.
    14	func TestCheckBinaryInstallLocationDetectsStaleCopy(t *testing.T) {
    15		runDir := t.TempDir()
    16		staleDir := t.TempDir()
    17	
    18		// "Running" binary.
    19		runningBin := filepath.Join(runDir, "ddx")
    20		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(runningBin, []byte("#!/bin/sh\necho running\n"), 0o755))
    21	
    22		// Stale binary placed earlier on PATH with different content → different SHA-256.
    23		staleBin := filepath.Join(staleDir, "ddx")
    24		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(staleBin, []byte("#!/bin/sh\necho stale\n"), 0o755))
    25	
    26		// Isolate PATH to only the test dirs so real ddx copies on the host don't interfere.
    27		t.Setenv("PATH", staleDir+string(os.PathListSeparator)+runDir)
    28	
    29		issues := checkBinaryInstallLocation(runningBin)
    30	
    31		var mismatch []DiagnosticIssue
    32		for _, issue := range issues {
    33			if issue.Type == "binary_sha_mismatch" {
    34				mismatch = append(mismatch, issue)
    35			}
    36		}
    37		require.Len(t, mismatch, 1, "expected exactly one sha_mismatch issue")
    38		assert.Contains(t, mismatch[0].Description, staleBin)
    39		assert.Contains(t, mismatch[0].Remediation[0], "rm")
    40		assert.Contains(t, mismatch[0].Remediation[0], staleBin)
    41		assert.Contains(t, mismatch[0].Remediation[0], runningBin)
    42	}
    43	
    44	// TestCheckBinaryInstallLocationNoFalsePositiveForSameSHA verifies that a copy of
    45	// the running binary with an identical SHA-256 is not flagged as stale.
    46	func TestCheckBinaryInstallLocationNoFalsePositiveForSameSHA(t *testing.T) {
    47		dir1 := t.TempDir()
    48		dir2 := t.TempDir()
    49	
    50		content := []byte("#!/bin/sh\necho ddx\n")
    51		bin1 := filepath.Join(dir1, "ddx")
    52		bin2 := filepath.Join(dir2, "ddx")
    53		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(bin1, content, 0o755))
    54		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(bin2, content, 0o755))
    55	
    56		t.Setenv("PATH", dir1+string(os.PathListSeparator)+dir2)
    57	
    58		issues := checkBinaryInstallLocation(bin1)
    59	
    60		for _, issue := range issues {
    61			assert.NotEqual(t, "binary_sha_mismatch", issue.Type,
    62				"same-SHA copy should not be flagged as stale")
    63		}
    64	}

    66	// TestCheckBinaryInstallLocationCanonicalPath verifies that a binary at
    67	// $HOME/.local/bin/ddx does not produce a binary_not_canonical issue.
    68	func TestCheckBinaryInstallLocationCanonicalPath(t *testing.T) {
    69		homeDir := t.TempDir()
    70		t.Setenv("HOME", homeDir)
    71	
    72		canonicalDir := filepath.Join(homeDir, ".local", "bin")
    73		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(canonicalDir, 0o755))
    74		canonicalBin := filepath.Join(canonicalDir, "ddx")
    75		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(canonicalBin, []byte("#!/bin/sh\necho ddx\n"), 0o755))
    76	
    77		t.Setenv("PATH", canonicalDir)
    78	
    79		issues := checkBinaryInstallLocation(canonicalBin)
    80	
    81		for _, issue := range issues {
    82			assert.NotEqual(t, "binary_not_canonical", issue.Type,
    83				"binary at canonical location should not produce a not_canonical issue")
    84		}
    85	}

    89	func TestCheckBinaryInstallLocationNonCanonicalWarns(t *testing.T) {
